description: Sentiment score from the AFINN lexicon applied to the message text (positive/negative valence)
Average number of characters per message or text entry.
Number of stopwords in the message, a proxy for verbosity or noise
Estimated proportion of English language content/messages, used to gate SNR and enable cross-region comparability
Count of message words after removing noise and stopwords; used for normalization/volume scaling
Fraction of message words matching financial vocabulary, indicating technical/informed content
Sentiment score from the Loughran–McDonald financial lexicon applied to the message
Count of numeric tokens in the message excluding years; proxy for technical/price-related chatter
Total number of punctuation marks found in the text.
Composite informativeness/signal-to-noise score for each StockTwits message indicating information richness or utility
Combined VADER compound sentiment score summarizing overall message polarity
Average VADER sentiment score for the message, tuned for social-media language
Count of tokens in the message that resemble years (e.g., 2024)
